breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 1.0 SCOPE AND FIELD OF APPLICATION
text: 1.0 SCOPE AND FIELD OF APPLICATION

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 1.0 SCOPE AND FIELD OF APPLICATION > Clause 1.1 The purpose of this specification is to indicate the general principles for inspecting the
text:
1.1 The purpose of this specification is to indicate the general principles for inspecting the
refractory materials, during and after production, and to obtain the most precise assessment of
the quality of a lot from a sample of the smallest possible size.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 1.0 SCOPE AND FIELD OF APPLICATION > Clause 1.2 The following standards have been referred to for the preparation of this specification:
text:
1.2 The following standards have been referred to for the preparation of this specification:
IS: 1528(PART-VII) Methods of sampling and physical tests for refractory
materials Part VII: Methods of sampling and criteria for
conformity.
1SO: 5022 Shaped Refractory Products — Sampling and
acceptance.
BS: 1902(Part 1A) Methods of Testing Refractory materials — Part 1A:
Sampling and physical tests.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 1.0 SCOPE AND FIELD OF APPLICATION > Clause 13 The details of tests for determination of various physical characteristics have not been
text:
13 The details of tests for determination of various physical characteristics have not been
included in this specification. The following standards shall be followed for this purpose:
BS: 1902(Part 1B) Basic Refractory Raw material and Fired/unfired
Refractories.
BS: 1902(Part 1C) Mouldable and Castable Refractories

The average for a sample (x) would differ from the true average for the whole lot ().

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES
text: 31 Lots

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES > Clause 3.1 The subdivision of a consignment into lots shall be in accordance with the following,
text:
3.1 The subdivision of a consignment into lots shall be in accordance with the following,
depending on the agreement between producer and consumer. Each lot shall then be sampled
and subjected to tests separately and accepted separately.
“
i) Subdivision of large consignment into small lots.
ii) Subdivision of consignment into lots on the basis of products belonging to different
classes or on the basis of different methods of manufacturer.
iii) Subdivision of consignment into lots according to sizes, masses or shapes of items.
iv) Subdivision of consignment into lots based on the period during which the item have
been manufactured.
The size of the lot in any of the cases given in clause 3.1.1 shall however, not exceed the
following limits:
- 100 Tonnes in the case of bricks and shapes;
- 50 Tonnes in the case of castables, ramming masses and plastic Refractories
and
- 5 Tonnes in the case of mortars.
If a lot is found not acceptable based on sampling and testing, the lot may be subdivided into
smaller sub-lots and the criteria for acceptance as given hereunder shall be applied, separately
for each sub lot. The later procedure shall be applied with mutual agreement between producer
and the consumer.
Sampling

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES > Clause 3.2.1 The purpose of a sample (a portion of material or a group of units) taken from a lot is to give
text:
3.2.1 The purpose of a sample (a portion of material or a group of units) taken from a lot is to give
information on the quality of the larger quantity.
322 The samples may be either random or representative.
Random samples are taken without bias so that every item has an equal chance of inclusion,
e.g. random samples may be taken by consumer from a consignment of refractory materials to
check the quality of the material.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES
text:
S5l - ENGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES
text:
Representative samples are obtained by systematically demarcating the lot into a convenient
number of equal portions and drawing samples from each portion at random to ensure that
each part of the sample represents a corresponding portion of the bulk, e.g. representative
sampling may be taken by manufacturer from a kiln to check the uniformity of firing.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 3.0 SUBDIVISION OF CONSIGNMENT INTO LOTS AND DRAWING OF SAMPLES
text:
323 The size of sample is determined by :
i) Statistical uniformity "of material (this necessitates a larger sampie when
dimensions are being investigated compared to when others such as refractoriness
are under consideration).
ii) Accuracy required in the test results.
iii) Nature of tests ( e.g. limitation on testing equipment)
iv) Size of lot.
General guidelines on size of sample for different tests is given in clause 4.0, “Inspection and
Criteria for Acceptance”

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE
text: 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > Clause 4.1 The inspection shall be in accordance with the purchase specification, which defines, for each
text:
4.1 The inspection shall be in accordance with the purchase specification, which defines, for each
class of refractory material, the following:
i) The properties on basis of which acceptance or rejection of lots will be decided.
i) The specified value for each of these properties and the relevant testing methods.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > Clause 4.2 The basis of inspection shall be either by “attributes” or by “variables” depending upon the
text:
4.2 The basis of inspection shall be either by “attributes” or by “variables” depending upon the
property under consideration.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes
text: 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes
text:
This method consists in taking note, for every item of a lot or of a sample taken from the lot,
of the presence or absence of certain qualitative characteristics (attribute) and in counting
how many items have or do not have this characteristic. This method shall be employed
while inspecting for non-destructive tests (e.g. inspection of external appearance or
inspection of dimensions). The maximum acceptable proportion of defective items (which
takes the form of the fixing of an Acceptable Quality Level: AQL) shall be as follows:

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes > 4.2.1.1 External appearance:
text: 4.2.1.1 External appearance:

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes > 4.2.1.1 External appearance:
text:
This inspection of external appearance shall cover the following visual defects: cracks,
blemishes, deformations, firing defects, etc. The AQL shall be taken as 4% in case of
ordinary bricks/mass produced items, and 1.5% for items having complicated shapes, unless
other wise specified in the purchase specification.

42.1.2" Dimensions
The dimensions shall also be inspected by attributes. For the purpose of this method, if the
dimensions of test piece lie outside the defined tolerance limits, the sample shall be taken as
defective. The AQL for this purpose shall be taken as 6.5%, unless otherwise specified in the
Purchase specification.
4213 Sampling Plan:

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR REFRACTORY MATERIALS- SAMPLING, INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > e NGINEERS STANDARD SPECIFICATION sTANDARD SPECIFICATION > 4.0 INSPECTION AND CRITERIA FOR ACCEPTANCE > 4.2.1 Inspection by Attributes > 4.2.1.1 External appearance: > Table I gives a selection of sampling plans for different AQLs.
text: 422 Inspection by Variables This method consists in measuring a quantitative characteristic for each item of a lot or a sample taken from this lot. This method shall be followed for inspecting the properties revealed by destructive tests(e.g. results of physical tests or of chemical analysis) and is based on the theoretical hypothesis that the property measured is distributed in the lot in accordance with normal law of statistics..* The details of the number of samples for Physical and Chemical tests of the characteristics to be covered under inspection by variables are given in Table 2. The sampling plan for inspection by variables shall depend on whether: a) guaranteed mean value for a particular characteristic is specified; or b) Fixed unilateral limit for individual values of the characteristics is specified. And shall be as per clause 4.2.2.1 or 4.2.2.2 respectively. *In practice, properties investigated will rarely be distributed precisely in accordance with this law, but the efficiency of the method of investigation is changed very little in cases where distributions deviate only slighty from normal. 4221 ‘When guaranteed value for the mean is specified For the lot to be acceptable: x < We+ fs (when [ specifies the upper limit) ... 32) And x 2 [l =fs (when [Lg specifies the lower limit) ... (3b) ‘Where s = Standard deviation of the sample of size n (calculated in accordance with equation 2). X = Observed mean of n individual values

4.2.22 Sampling plan when fixed unilateral limit for individual values is specified
For the lot to be acceptable:
x < L —ks (when L specifies the upper limit) ........................4(a)
And x > L + ks (when L specifies the lower limit .................. 4(b)
Where s = Standard deviation of the sample of size n (calculated in
accordance with equation 2).
X = Observed mean of n individual values of the sample (calculated
in accordance with equation 1).
k = Acceptance factor (from Table 4).
L = Specified upper/lower limit of individual value.
